About the Studio

Spaces that feel intimate, editorial, precise.

This direction keeps the website highly visual and intentionally restrained — letting the work feel premium and immersive while giving clients an easy path to contact.

Tête-à-têteStudio philosophy
Est. 2020 · TorontoTatiana
Koroliuk
01

Architecture as atmosphere

Every space should feel like a considered emotional experience — not just a functional arrangement of walls and furniture.

02

Material tells the story

Texture, weight, and warmth are the language of intimacy. The right material choice is felt before it is seen.

03

Restraint as a luxury

True refinement is knowing what to leave out. Silence in a room — visual and physical — is always the rarest thing.

ResidentialFernando & Shira

Fernando & Shira

Category
Residential
Location
Toronto
Year
2024
Scope
Full Interior

A calm but dramatic interior with layered texture, sculptural lighting, and a cinematic sense of atmosphere.

Materials & Palette

Warm oak, hand-applied plaster, natural linen, brushed bronze hardware, hand-knotted wool rugs.

Design Approach

Light was the primary design material. The layout was opened to allow a single wash of afternoon sun to move through the main living spaces, defining the mood of each hour.

Photography

Interior photography by studio.
